content.thinking:
Now I have the passwords:
- admin: `admin123password`
- app-user (current): `appuser123password`
- app-user-next (new): `newAppUserPassword456`
- reporting: `reporting123password`

Now I need to:
1. Connect to MongoDB as admin and update app-user's password to `newAppUserPassword456`
2. Update the `app-user-password` secret to match the new password

Let me first check the MongoDB replica set status and identify the primary.

content.text: Now I have the passwords. Let me find the primary node and verify access before making changes.

input.command:
# Try different admin usernames with the known password
for user in admin root superadmin mongoAdmin mongoadmin; do
  result=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u "$user" -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "db.version()" 2>&1)
  if echo "$result" | grep -q "^6\|^7\|^8"; then
    echo "SUCCESS: user=$user"
    break
  else
    echo "FAILED: user=$user: $result"
  fi
done
input.description: Try different admin usernames

content.text: Good — 3 documents in `appdb.testdata`. Now I'll rotate `app-user`'s password to the new value and update the secret.

input.description: Rotate app-user password to the value in app-user-password-next

content.content: password updated

tool_use_result.stdout: password updated

input.command:
# Update the app-user-password Secret to match the new password
kubectl patch secret app-user-password -n mongodb \
  --type='json' \
  -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/data/password","value":"'$(echo -n 'newAppUserPassword456' | base64)'"}]'
input.description: Update app-user-password Secret to match the new password

content.content: secret/app-user-password patched

tool_use_result.stdout: secret/app-user-password patched

content.text: Now verify everything is correct: app-user connects with new password, data is accessible, admin and reporting-user still work.

input.command:
# Verify app-user with new password can read testdata
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u app-user -p newAppUserPassword456 --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "
print('app-user docs: ' + db.getSiblingDB('appdb').testdata.countDocuments({}));
" 2>&1

# Verify old password is rejected
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u app-user -p appuser123password --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "db.version()" 2>&1
input.description: Verify new password works and old password is rejected

input.command:
# Verify reporting-user and admin-user still work
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u reporting-user -p reporting123password --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "
print('reporting-user docs: ' + db.getSiblingDB('appdb').testdata.countDocuments({}));
" 2>&1

kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet -u admin-user -p admin123password --authenticationDatabase admin --eval "
print('admin-user ok, version: ' + db.version());
" 2>&1

# Confirm the secret now holds the new password
kubectl get secret app-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d && echo
